dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3414 IS: Justice for Breonna Taylor Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Paul (for himself and Mr. Booker) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit no-knock warrants, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Justice for Breonna Taylor Act.

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a Federal law enforcement officer (as defined in section 115 of title 18, United States Code) may not execute a warrant until after the officer provides notice of his or her authority and purpose.

subsection.header: State and local law enforcement agencies
subsection.text: Beginning in the first fiscal year beginning after the date of enactment of this Act, and each fiscal year thereafter, a State or local law enforcement agency that receives funds from the Department of Justice during the fiscal year may not execute a warrant that does not require the law enforcement officer serving the warrant to provide notice of his or her authority and purpose before forcibly entering a premises.
